crash_signature.context: 100+ sequential RPC calls while mining active
crash_signature.interpretation: Use-after-free deref of struct field at offset 0x80 — recurring layout suggests Block, PeerConnection, or WsClient

rank_1.description: Race on P2PNode.peers ArrayList between RPC handler threads (handlePeers iterates) and accept thread (acceptLoop appends)
evidence: P2PNode has zero mutex on self.peers (verified via grep — no peers_mutex member, no lock() calls)
self.peers.append() at p2p.zig:922 has no synchronization
self.peers.swapRemove() at p2p.zig:1158 has no synchronization
Crash occurs immediately after RPC server starts under stress

rank_2.description: Race on Blockchain.chain between mining loop (chain.append) and unlocked RPC handlers (getlatestblock, getbestblockhash)
evidence: blockchain.zig:1019 getLatestBlock returns Block by value with live heap-borrowed fields (.hash slice, .transactions.items pointer)
rpc_server.zig handleGetLatestBlock and handleGetBestBlockHash do NOT acquire ctx.bc.mutex
Other handlers in same file DO acquire mutex (e.g. handleGetBalance line 310, handleGetTx line 481) — inconsistency confirms oversight
Mining loop runs every 100ms-1s and mutates chain

top_3_priority_fixes.title: Add peers_mutex to P2PNode and lock all self.peers access
top_3_priority_fixes.fix: Introduce peers_mutex: std.Thread.Mutex = .{}; lock around append/swapRemove and every read iterator. Promote inbound_count/outbound_count to std.atomic.Value(u32).

top_3_priority_fixes.title: Acquire bc.mutex in unlocked RPC handlers
top_3_priority_fixes.fix: Pattern: lock; snapshot scalars + dupe hash strings to local buffers; unlock; allocPrint outside lock.

top_3_priority_fixes.title: Make getLatestBlock lock-aware or return BlockSnapshot
top_3_priority_fixes.fix: Option A: assert mutex held in debug. Option B (preferred): introduce BlockSnapshot POD with copied scalars + [64]u8 hash buffers; getLatestBlock takes lock internally and returns snapshot.

sites.description: Returns Block by value with live heap-borrowed fields. Block.transactions.items pointer + Block.hash slice can dangle if mining appends/replaces during caller use.
sites.suggested_fix: Require caller hold self.mutex, or refactor to return BlockSnapshot deep-copy.

sites.description: Calls bc.getLatestBlock() then dereferences blk.hash/blk.previous_hash/blk.transactions.items.len without bc.mutex while mining loop concurrently mutates chain.
sites.suggested_fix: Lock bc.mutex; copy scalars + dupe hash to local buf; unlock; format.

sites.description: Reads blk.hash slice from latest block without bc.mutex.
Iterates p2p.peers.items reading peer.node_id/peer.host while accept thread can append/realloc. Use-after-free of items pointer.
sites.suggested_fix: Lock p2p.peers_mutex (to be added); snapshot peer info; unlock; format.

sites.description: self.peers.append(conn) — no synchronization. Realloc invalidates readers. outbound_count += 1 also racy.
sites.suggested_fix: Add peers_mutex; lock here; promote counters to atomics.

sites.description: swapRemove(i) on self.peers without lock — invalidates concurrent iterators.
sites.suggested_fix: Hold peers_mutex for the entire while loop, or batch removals under lock.
Lock both mutexes; snapshot lengths; unlock.

sites.description: node.inbound_count += 1 / -= 1 across multiple handler threads — racy r-m-w on plain u32. Also read by canAcceptInbound() — possible MAX_INBOUND bypass.
sites.suggested_fix: Promote inbound_count to std.atomic.Value(u32); use fetchAdd/fetchSub(.monotonic).

sites.description: Writes node.chain_height and peer.height from per-peer threads without sync. Also reads node.blockchain.?.chain.items.len unlocked.
sites.suggested_fix: Use std.atomic.Value(u64) for chain_height; acquire bc.mutex when reading chain.items.len.

sites.description: stream.close() and connected=false executed BEFORE srv.mutex.lock(). broadcast() may snapshot the client pointer under lock and call sendText after handler thread destroys the WsClient struct → use-after-free.
sites.suggested_fix: Take srv.mutex first; do close + remove + destroy entirely under lock.

sites.description: Returns chain.items.len without lock. Single-read torn issues unlikely on x86-64 but encourages callers to chain unlocked count+access pairs.
sites.suggested_fix: Document that callers must hold bc.mutex when correlating count with subsequent chain index access.

sites.description: MAX_CONCURRENT=4 RPC threads + mining + WS broadcast share GPA. GPA in 0.15.2 has thread_safe default true, so safe but contended.
sites.suggested_fix: Switch hot RPC path to per-request arena allocator; reset on response send.

false-positives-considered.candidate: RPCThreadArgs passed by value to Thread.spawn (main.zig:530)
false-positives-considered.verdict: NOT a bug — only contains pointers to objects whose lifetime is main() itself (never returns while threads run).
All 6 occurrences are @alignCast(@ptrCast(...)) round-trips between matching opaque pointer ABIs — safe.
Default config in Zig 0.15.2 has thread_safe = !single_threaded, so concurrent use is correct (mutex-guarded). Not the cause.
